zh.lifeSummary: Margred Turberville於1403年出生在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根。她於1423年與Sir William John Treherne在英國格盧斯特斯爾科華士 本 迪恩結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1425年在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾科華士 本 迪恩享年22歲。

nameMeanings.name: Turberville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name, probably from Thouberville (Eure), or possibly from Trubleville (Seine-Maritime), both in France. The two may have been confused in surname usage.

variants.name: Torbet
meanings: from the Anglo-Norman and Middle English personal name T(h)urbert, T(h)orbert, a hybrid of Old Norse Thorr ‘Thor’, the name of the god of thunder, + ancient Germanic -berht ‘bright, famous’. The name was probably formed on the Continent; see Turberville .
perhaps in some cases a nickname from Old French and Middle English turbot, turbut ‘turbot’, for someone who caught or sold or resembled this fish.

variants.name: Tuberville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Turberville  . This form of the surname is very rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Turville
meanings: English (Surrey and Hampshire; of Norman origin):
habitational name from Tourville‐la‐Campagne (Eure, France). The placename derives from the Old Norse personal name Thórr + Old French ville ‘farmstead, estate’ (from Latin villa).
occasionally a shortened form of   Turberville  .

variants.name: Troublefield
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Turberville  .

nameMeanings.name: Margred
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Margaret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Margred Turberville was born in 1403, in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom. She married Sir William John Treherne in 1423, in Forest of Dean, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1425, in Forest of Dean, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 22.

ja.lifeSummary: Margred Turbervilleは1403年にイギリスウェールズグラモーガンで生まれた。1423年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアフォーエスト オフ ディーンでSir William John Treherneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1425年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアフォーエスト オフ ディーンで22歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margred Turberville(은)는 1403년 영국 웨일스 글램르건에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1423년 영국 그루스스터실르 포레스트 프 디에서 Sir William John Treherne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1425년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 포레스트 프 디에서 22세의 나이로 사망했다.
